name: Surrounding the Vega de Granada — circular from Gabia Grande
name_original: Rodeando la Vega de Granada — circular desde Gabia Grande
region_intro: Hiking around Las Gabias offers diverse terrain, situated in the fertile Vega of Granada and at the foot of the Sierra Nevada. The region features irrigated plains fed by the Dílar River, alongside drier areas characterized by olive groves. While the municipality itself is at a lower altitude, it provides access to the rugged trails and varied ecosystems of the nearby Sierra Nevada mountain range. This blend of riverine landscapes, agricultural fields, and elevated backdrops creates varied hiking opportunities. Best hiking trails around Las Gabias The most popular hiking route is Santa Fe Hot Springs Loop, an 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete, leading through varied landscapes including hot springs. Another top favourite among local hikers is Elephant Rock – Armilla Air Base loop from Armilla, a moderate 8.5 miles (13.6 km) path. This route offers a loop through the local countryside, passing by the Armilla Air Base. Local hikers also love the Hot Springs – Santa Fe Hot Springs loop from Hoya de los Frailes, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through the area's natural hot springs, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Hiking around Las Gabias is defined by fertile plains, dry land with olive groves, and views towards the Sierra Nevada. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Las Gabias are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 3000 reviews. More than 15000 hikers have used komoot to explore Las Gabias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Las Gabias?
A: Las Gabias boasts an extensive network of over 390 hiking routes. This includes more than 160 easy trails, over 200 moderate options, and around 25 more challenging routes, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on a hike near Las Gabias?
A: Hiking around Las Gabias offers a diverse mix of landscapes. You'll traverse fertile irrigated plains fed by the Dílar River, explore drier areas characterized by olive groves, and enjoy remarkable panoramic views of the Sierra Nevada mountains. The region also features several streams and ravines, adding to the varied scenery.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Las Gabias?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e978080129/santa-fe-hot-springs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Hot Springs Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 14.3 km circular trail that takes you through varied landscapes, including natural hot springs. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33908994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elephant Rock – Armilla Air Base loop from Armilla&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.6 km circular path through the local countryside.

Q: Can I find easy or family-friendly hikes in Las Gabias?
A: Absolutely. Las Gabias is well-suited for families and beginners, with over 160 easy routes available. These trails typically feature gentle terrain and shorter distances, perfect for a relaxed outing. While specific family-friendly durations vary, many easy routes can be completed in a few hours.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural and historical points of interest. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e978080129/santa-fe-hot-springs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Hot Springs Loop&lt;/a&gt;, lead directly to the natural &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5511812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find historical sites like the Torre Nazarí and a Roman Villa nearby, offering a blend of nature and culture.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Las Gabias?
A: The hiking trails around Las Gabias are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 3000 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful views towards the Sierra Nevada, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites to explore near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Las Gabias offers several historical and cultural sites. You can visit the 14th-century Torre Nazarí, explore a Roman Villa, or see the 16th-century Ermita de Nuestra Señora de las Nieves. These landmarks provide fascinating insights into the region's past, dating back to Roman and Moorish times.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Las Gabias?
A: The diverse terrain of Las Gabias makes it suitable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the plains and olive groves. Even in winter, many lower-altitude trails remain accessible, providing a different perspective on the landscape, often with clear views of the snow-capped Sierra Nevada.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Las Gabias?
A: While specific regulations can vary by trail, many outdoor areas in Las Gabias are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in agricultural areas or near wildlife, and to carry waste bags to maintain the cleanliness of the trails.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the hikes?
A: Yes, the proximity to the Sierra Nevada ensures many routes offer stunning panoramic views. The iconic Montevive hill, closely linked to the area, also provides elevated perspectives. You can also find specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/380032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Nicolás Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offers spectacular vistas of Granada and the Alhambra.

Q: How can I get to the hiking trails using public transport?
A: Las Gabias is connected to Granada by local bus services, which can help you reach the municipality. From there, access to specific trailheads might require a short walk or a local taxi, as public transport options directly to every starting point can be limited. Planning your route in advance with local transport schedules is advisable.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Las Gabias?
A: Parking availability varies depending on the specific trailhead. In and around the main town of Las Gabias, you can usually find public parking. For trails starting in smaller villages or more rural areas, roadside parking might be an option, but it's always best to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking information.

Santa Fe is a municipality in the province of Granada, in the autonomous community of Andalusia. Located in the Vega de Granada and crossed by the Genil river from east to west, the municipal area is almost completely flat and lacks mountainous elevations.
